name: c
help: claudewheel - TUI launcher for Claude Code with profile, model, and directory selection

health.name: health
health.help: run diagnostic health checks on profiles, tokens, and hooks, then exit

config.name: config
config.help: open the ~/.claudewheel/ config directory in your $EDITOR

versions.name: versions
versions.help: list all installed Claude Code versions, marking the current symlink target

install.name: install
install.help: download and install a specific Claude Code version

args.name: version
args.help: semver version string to download and install (e.g. 2.1.119)

uninstall.name: uninstall
uninstall.help: delete an installed Claude Code version binary from the versions directory

args.name: version
args.help: semver version string to remove (refuses if it is the current symlink target)

reset-options.name: reset-options
reset-options.help: delete options.json so it regenerates from defaults

show.name: show
show.help: print a summary of current segment selections, theme, and recent directories

migrate.name: migrate
migrate.help: move session data files from one profile to another, optionally filtered by UUID

args.name: src
args.help: source profile name whose sessions will be moved (e.g. work)

args.name: dst
args.help: destination profile name to receive the migrated sessions (e.g. personal)

args.name: uuid
args.help: optional UUID substring to migrate only matching sessions

stats.name: stats
stats.help: report shared-store stats and clean up legacy data

flags.name: dry-run
flags.help: preview cleanup changes without writing anything to disk

mv.name: mv
mv.help: rename a project directory and migrate session data

flags.name: dry-run
flags.help: preview the rename and session migration without writing anything to disk

flags.name: post-hoc
flags.help: skip filesystem rename, migrate sessions only (directory already renamed)

args.name: old
args.help: current path of the project directory to rename (absolute or relative)

args.name: new
args.help: target path for the renamed project directory (absolute or relative)

import.name: import
import.help: import session data from an external Claude Code directory

flags.name: dry-run
flags.help: preview the import operation without writing any session data to disk

flags.name: reid
flags.help: assign new UUIDs to sessions that collide with existing local sessions

flags.name: from
flags.help: original project path as recorded in the source session data (repeatable)

flags.name: to
flags.help: local directory path that corresponds to the --from path on this machine (repeatable)

deploy-hooks.name: deploy-hooks
deploy-hooks.help: deploy built-in hook scripts to the ~/.claudewheel/scripts/ directory

flags.name: all
flags.help: deploy every known hook script from the built-in registry at once

flags.name: force-overwrite
flags.help: overwrite existing hook scripts on disk instead of skipping them

args.name: name
args.help: name of the specific hook script to deploy (omit to use --all)

patch-profiles.name: patch-profiles
patch-profiles.help: sync existing profiles and shared-settings.json to canonical hook and disallowedTools defaults

flags.name: dry-run
flags.help: preview the changes without writing anything to disk

reconcile-permissions.name: reconcile-permissions
reconcile-permissions.help: reconcile profile and shared-settings permissions (deny/ask/allow) to the canonical guardrail model; requires exactly one of --dry-run or --apply

flags.name: dry-run
flags.help: print the per-target permissions diff and change NOTHING (mutually exclusive with --apply; you MUST pass exactly one of --dry-run or --apply)

flags.name: apply
flags.help: perform the reconciliation, writing each target atomically (mutually exclusive with --dry-run; you MUST pass exactly one of --dry-run or --apply)

flags.name: profile
flags.help: reconcile only this single profile; when given, shared-settings.json profileDefaults is left untouched (omit to reconcile every profile AND shared-settings profileDefaults)

launch.name: launch
launch.help: start the interactive TUI launcher to select a profile, model, and directory

flags.name: cont
flags.help: continue the most recent conversation in the current directory

flags.name: resume
flags.help: resume a specific session by its UUID, or pass empty string to open the picker

flags.name: print-prompt
flags.help: run in non-interactive print mode with the given prompt

flags.name: picker
flags.help: open the interactive session resume picker to browse and select a session

flags.name: profile
flags.help: preset the Profile segment to this value, skipping TUI selection for it

flags.name: github
flags.help: preset the GitHub account segment to this value, skipping TUI selection for it

flags.name: model
flags.help: preset the Model segment to this value (e.g. opus, sonnet), skipping TUI selection

flags.name: directory
flags.help: preset the Directory segment to this path, skipping TUI selection for it

flags.name: mcp
flags.help: preset the MCP mode segment to this value, skipping TUI selection for it

flags.name: permissions
flags.help: preset the Permissions segment to this value, skipping TUI selection for it

flags.name: set
flags.help: set any segment value as KEY=VALUE (e.g. -s version=2.1.119); repeatable

flags.name: client
flags.help: launch target client (one of: claude, miniclaude). When omitted, the interactive launcher prompts with a Client step (cursor on config default_client, else claude) and non-interactive launches use default_client. Passing it explicitly skips that step. 'miniclaude' launches the miniclaude REPL instead; version and strict-MCP selections, config default_flags, and the disallowedTools list are claude-client-only

profile.name: profile
profile.help: create, inspect, rename, delete, and manage Claude Code profiles and their stored tokens

create.name: create
create.help: create a new profile interactively through a guided wizard, then set up its authentication

delete.name: delete
delete.help: delete a registered profile and clean up its directory, tokens, and options entries

flags.name: force-delete
flags.help: force deletion even if sessions appear active; skips the safety check

flags.name: force-delete-data
flags.help: delete even when shared-dir names hold REAL data instead of symlinks; this DESTROYS that data (e.g. conversation history)

args.name: name
args.help: name of the profile to delete (e.g. work, personal, lisa)

show.name: show
show.help: inspect a profile's configuration, authentication status, and session data in a detailed report

args.name: name
args.help: name of the profile to inspect (e.g. work, personal, default)

rename.name: rename
rename.help: rename a profile, moving its directory, tokens, and session data to the new name

args.name: old
args.help: current name of the profile to rename (must be an existing, non-running profile)

args.name: new
args.help: new name for the profile (lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens; must be unused)

fix-auth.name: fix-auth
fix-auth.help: remove session credentials that shadow a long-lived token

args.name: name
args.help: name of the profile whose shadowing session credentials should be removed

check-tokens.name: check-tokens
check-tokens.help: validate every discovered profile's stored OAuth token against the Anthropic API

permission.name: permission
permission.help: add, remove, and list permission rules across Claude profiles

add.name: add
add.help: Add a permission rule to a profile's settings.json. Takes a category (allow, deny, or ask) and a rule string such as Bash or Read(//home/**). Writes the rule into the specified category array. Use --profile to target a single profile or --all-profiles to apply the rule across every registered profile. Skips duplicates if the rule already exists in the category.

flags.name: profile
flags.help: target a specific profile by name (mutually exclusive with --all-profiles)

flags.name: all-profiles
flags.help: apply the operation to every registered profile at once

args.name: category
args.help: permission category to add the rule to: allow, deny, or ask

args.name: rule
args.help: permission rule string to add (e.g. Bash, Read(//home/**), Edit)

remove.name: remove
remove.help: Remove a permission rule from a profile's settings.json. Takes a category (allow, deny, or ask) and the exact rule string to delete. The rule is removed from the specified category array and the file is saved. Use --profile to target a single profile or --all-profiles to remove the rule from every registered profile. Reports whether the rule was found.

flags.name: profile
flags.help: target a specific profile by name (mutually exclusive with --all-profiles)

flags.name: all-profiles
flags.help: apply the operation to every registered profile at once

args.name: category
args.help: permission category to remove the rule from: allow, deny, or ask

args.name: rule
args.help: exact permission rule string to remove (must match an existing entry)

list.name: list
list.help: List permission rules from a profile's settings.json. Displays rules in grouped, flat, or JSON format controlled by --format. Use --category to filter output to a single category (allow, deny, or ask). Use --profile to inspect a single profile or --all-profiles to show rules from every registered profile, with each profile's rules displayed under a header.

flags.name: format
flags.help: output format: grouped (indented tree), flat (tsv), or json

flags.name: category
flags.help: restrict output to a single permission category (allow, deny, or ask)

flags.name: profile
flags.help: target a specific profile by name (mutually exclusive with --all-profiles)

flags.name: all-profiles
flags.help: apply the operation to every registered profile at once
